original_abstract:
This volume offers a comprehensive appraisal of the Russo-Ukrainian War, employing the framework of "resilient fighting power" to evaluate the operational performance of both combatants during the first year of the 2022 full-scale invasion. While the conflict originated in 2014, the escalation on February 24, 2022, transformed it into a high-intensity inter-state struggle of a scale unseen in Europe for generations, reigniting fundamental debates regarding modern conventional warfare. 

Rather than striving for an exhaustive chronological record, this book provide a systematic analysis across the land, air, maritime, and cyber domains. It begins by exploring the conceptual foundations of the conflict, specifically how the Ukrainian and Russian Armed Forces have adapted their shared Soviet strategic heritage in the post-Cold War era. The analysis further examines the critical role of international intervention—including military aid, sanctions, and technology transfers—in bolstering Ukrainian resilience. Concluding with a reflection on the shifting nature of inter-state conflict, this work serves as an essential resource for students and scholars of military and strategic studies, defense policy, and international relations. 

The Open Access version of this book, available at http://www.taylorfrancis.com, has been made available under a Creative Commons Attribution-Non Commercial-No Derivatives (CC-BY-NC-ND) 4.0 license.
humanized_abstract:
This book provides the first thorough assessment of the entire Russo-Ukrainian War using the fighting power resilience framework to evaluate the performance of Russian and Ukrainian troops in the first year of the 2022 war. The conflict has its origins in 2014, but on 24 February 2022 the war was brought into its present phase of intense inter-state conflict, after many decades in which Europe had seen none, raising in tragic circumstances all the questions that have been asked in recent years about the nature of modern conventional warfare.

The war in eastern Ukraine is often portrayed as an unremarkable or even indeterminate conflict. Covering every aspect of the conflict in exhaustive detail over time is impractical, so this book instead explores the war in depth across the various domains of warfare – land, air, sea and cyber. First, the book delves into the theoretical roots of the war and how the military doctrine of the Ukrainian and Russian Armed Forces has adapted the shared Soviet legacy of war fighting concepts in the post-Cold War period. It then examines the impact of foreign military assistance, sanctions and technology transfer on the Ukrainian Armed Forces' ability to resist the Russian invasion. The book concludes by reflecting on the nature of interstate warfare in the 21st century. This book will be of interest to students and scholars of military and strategic studies, defence policy and international relations.

This TB textbook is published Open Access with a Creative Commons Attribution-Non Commercial-No Derivatives (CC-BY-NC-ND) 4.0 license. The full texts of the Open Access version are available at http://www.taylorfrancis.com.
